body:
This review focuses on issues not already raised in the previous review. I found two additional concrete problems: a SQL-injection vector and a docstring/implementation mismatch. Once those (and the already-flagged unused imports, bare `except`, and mutable default) are addressed, this throwaway script will be internally consistent.

_This review was created by an AI agent (OpenHands) on behalf of a user._

comments.body: 🟠 Important: The docstring says the match should be case-insensitive, but the comparison is case-sensitive (`rec["name"] == name`). Also, the docstring implies `rec` is a dict, yet the prior buggy iteration pattern would iterate the string if `records` were a string. Make the comparison `name.lower() == rec["name"].lower()` or normalize once outside the loop.

🔴 Critical: String interpolation of `user_id` and `table` into a SQL fragment enables SQL injection. Even as a fragment, this pattern should use parameterized placeholders (e.g., `WHERE user_id = ? AND table = ?`) and let the caller bind values.
